summary.intent: Address a third round of Codex review findings on PR #669 in the ars-ui repo, specifically fixing blur key-preservation, passive SyncProps opening, and closed-popup Enter behavior in the combobox component.
summary.outcome: Three regression tests were written, verified to fail, then fixed. Local gates (fmt, clippy, tests, llvm-cov) passed. Fixes were committed to combobox/mod.rs and pushed, with Codex threads replied to, resolved, and a new @codex review triggered.

repo: The combobox component lives at crates/ars-components/src/selection/combobox/mod.rs and has a rich local test suite exercised via `cargo test -p ars-components selection::combobox`.
The review loop with Codex uses GitHub reactions (eyes = reviewing) and unresolved thread count as state signals, polled via `gh api graphql` on a ~2-minute cadence.

CI matrix for this repo includes: snapshot, formatting, clippy, unit tests, I18n, browser checks, coverage, Feature Flags Dioxus, and Release Verification — several run in parallel and complete at different times.
Codex is mentioned as a co-author in commits: `Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>`.

code.finding: Blur handler was rewriting the selected item's key to its label — fix preserves the key on blur for custom-value-allowed comboboxes.
Passive SyncProps was incorrectly triggering popup open on an empty-allowed combobox — fix gates the open on active user interaction.
Enter key while popup is closed was not selecting the highlighted item — fix routes closed-state Enter through the selection path.

workflow: TDD approach used: write failing regression tests first, confirm they fail against current code, then implement the fix and confirm they pass.
`cargo fmt` must be run before committing — `cargo fmt --check` will fail CI if skipped, even for cosmetic multiline if-let layout differences.
Polling strategy: `sleep 120 && gh api ...` chained in a single shell command to batch the wait and the state fetch, avoiding extra round-trips.
Codex review state is tracked by: (1) presence of eyes reaction on the PR comment, (2) count of unresolved Codex-authored threads via GraphQL.

friction: Multiple polling cycles (10+) were needed for a single Codex review pass, with no output during sleep windows — the loop is slow and opaque.
`cargo fmt` introduced a formatting change post-fix that required an extra gate cycle before committing.
CI matrix is large and staggered; several gates (Coverage, Feature Flags Dioxus, Release Verification) consistently lag behind the others, extending the wait before a clean green state.
open_items: A fourth @codex review pass was triggered at end of session — outcome (approval or further findings) is not yet known.
Coverage, Feature Flags Dioxus, and Release Verification CI gates were still pending when the session ended.
The iterative fix-loop pattern (fix → push → @codex review → repeat) may benefit from batching more findings per cycle to reduce total round-trips.
